rules: Look at your opponent's hand!

attacks.name: Thrash
attacks.effect: If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage. If tails, Gyarados does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: They say that during past strife, Gyarados would appear and leave blazing ruins in its wake.

attacks.name: Elemental Blast
attacks.effect: Discard a Fire Energy, Water Energy, and Lightning Energy attached to Lugia LEGEND.

rules: Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Lugia LEGEND.
flavorText: It is said to be the guardian of the seas. It is rumored to have been seen on the night of a storm.

attacks.name: Elemental Blast
attacks.effect: Discard a Fire Energy, Water Energy, and Lightning Energy attached to Lugia LEGEND.

rules: Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Lugia LEGEND.
flavorText: It is said to be the guardian of the seas. It is rumored to have been seen on the night of a storm.

attacks.name: Bright Wing
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy attached to Ho-Oh LEGEND.

rules: Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Ho-Oh LEGEND.
flavorText: Legends claim this Pokémon flies the world's skies continuously on its magnificent, seven-colored wings.

attacks.name: Bright Wing
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy attached to Ho-Oh LEGEND.

rules: Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Ho-Oh LEGEND.
flavorText: Legends claim this Pokémon flies the world's skies continuously on its magnificent, seven-colored wings.

attacks.name: Flare Destroy
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy card attached to Typhlosion and discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hydro Crunch
attacks.effect: Does 60 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Lightning Crush
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 40 more damage. If tails, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

rules: Attach Rainbow Energy to 1 of your Pokémon. While in play, Rainbow Energy provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time. (Has no effect other than providing Energy.) When you attach this card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on that Pokémon. (While not in play, Rainbow Energy counts as Colorless Energy.)
Double Colorless Energy provides ColorlessColorless Energy.
Switch 1 of your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

fullDisplayName: Professor Oak's New Theory 101/123 HeartGold SoulSilver
rules:
You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.

fullDisplayName: Professor Elm's Training Method 100/123 HeartGold SoulSilver
rules:
You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Search your deck for an Evolution card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon, and switch it with your opponent's Active Pokémon.

Choose 1 Pokémon in your hand, show it to your opponent, and put it on top of your deck. If you do, search your deck for a Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. Choose a Supporter card you find there, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Choose 1 of your Pokémon. Flip 2 coins. For each heads, remove 3 damage counters from that Pokémon.
Remove all Special Conditions from your Active Pokémon.

You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Search your discard pile for 4 basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand.
Move a basic Energy card attached 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a number of cards equal to the number of cards in your opponent's hand.

You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Tail Whip
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: When it walks around on the ground, it coats its body with a slimy, poisonous film.

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy attached to Vulpix.

flavorText: If it is attacked by an enemy that is stronger than itself, it feigns injury to fool the enemy and escapes.
Its powerful, well-developed jaws are capable of crushing anything. Even its trainer must be careful.

attacks.name: Cure Kernels
attacks.effect: Remove 2 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: It may plummet from the sky. If attacked by a Spearow, it will violently shake its leaves.
At night, the center of its body slowly flickers with the same rhythm as a human heartbeat.

attacks.name: Spider Web
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It lies still in the same pose for days in its web, waiting for its unsuspecting prey to wander close.

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It has an active, playful nature. Many women like to frolic with it because of its affectionate ways.

attacks.name: Whimsy Tackle
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It lazes vacantly near water. If something bites its tail, it won't even notice for a whole day.

attacks.name: Scout
attacks.effect: Look at your opponent's hand.

flavorText: A very cautious Pokémon, it raises itself up using its tail to get a better view of its surroundings.

attacks.name: Defense Curl
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Sandshrew by attacks during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Disliking water, it lives in deep burrows in arid areas. It can roll itself instantly into a ball.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: It raises its tail to check its surroundings. The tail is sometimes struck by lightning in this pose.

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Phanpy.

flavorText: It swings its long snout around playfully, but because it is so strong, that can be dangerous.

attacks.name: Double-edge Claw
attacks.effect: Paras does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: As its body grows large, large mushrooms named tochukaso start sprouting out of its back.

attacks.name: Pay Day
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

flavorText: It loves anything that shines. It especially adores coins that it picks up and secretly hoards.

attacks.name: Water Splash
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: The end of its tail serves as a buoy that keeps it from drowning, even in a vicious current.

attacks.name: Static Electricity
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a number of Lightning Energy cards up to the number of Mareep in play (both yours and your opponent's) and attach them to Mareep. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: If static electricity builds in its body, its fleece doubles in volume. Touching it will shock you.
For no reason, it jumps and splashes about, making it easy for predators like Pidgeotto to catch it mid-jump.

attacks.name: Comet Punch
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: When the weather turns cold, lots of Ledyba gather from everywhere to cluster and keep each other warm.

attacks.name: Smokescreen
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: Its thin, filmy body is filled with gases that cause constant sniffles, coughs and teary eyes.

attacks.name: Mimic
attacks.effect: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a number of cards equal to the number of cards in your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Lick
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It rocks its body rhythmically. It appears to alter the rhythm depending on how it is feeling.

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Looking into its cute, round eyes causes it to sing a relaxing melody, inducing its enemies to sleep.

attacks.name: Bounce
attacks.effect: You may switch Hoppip with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: To keep from being blown away by the wind, they gather in clusters. But they do enjoy gentle breezes.

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It always stands on one foot. It changes feet so fast, the movement can rarely be seen.
It has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.

attacks.name: Show Off
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Its tail has a small brain of its own. Beware! If you get close, it may react to your scent and bite.

attacks.name: Continuous Eggsplosion
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Using telepathy only they can employ, they always form a cluster of six Exeggcute.

attacks.name: Sleep Inducer
attacks.effect: Switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. The new Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It remembers every dream it eats. It rarely eats the dreams of adults because children's are much tastier.
It usually stays hunched over. If it is angry or surprised, it shoots flames out of its back.

attacks.name: Minimize
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Clefairy by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: The moonlight that it stores in the wings on its back apparently gives it the ability to float in midair.
Its pleasantly aromatic leaf has the ability to check the humidity and temperature.

attacks.name: Happy Punch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, remove 3 damage counters from Chansey.

flavorText: It walks carefully to prevent its egg from breaking. However, it is extremely fast at running away.
Its feet have suction cups designed to stick to any surface. It tenaciously climbs trees to forage.

attacks.name: Double Slap
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Expand
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Wigglytuff by attacks is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It has a very fine fur. Take care not to make it angry, or it may inflate steadily and hit with a Body Slam.
Its flat, thin body is always stuck on walls. Its shape appears to have some meaning.
Their shapes look like hieroglyphs on ancient tablets. It is said that the two are somehow related.

attacks.name: Cosmic Cyclone
attacks.effect: Choose as many Water Energy attached to your Pokémon as you like. This attack does 20 damage times the number of Energy you chose. Shuffle those cards back into your deck.

flavorText: The middle section of its body is called the core. It glows in a different color each time it is seen.

attacks.name: Big Yawn
attacks.effect: Both Slowbro and the Defending Pokémon are now Asleep.

attacks.name: Madkinesis
attacks.effect: Does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each Psychic Energy attached to Slowbro.

flavorText: If the tail-biting Shellder is thrown off in a harsh battle, it reverts to being an ordinary Slowpoke.

attacks.name: Knock Away
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: The bloom on top of its head opens and closes as the temperature fluctuates up and down.

attacks.name: Offensive Needle
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: To fire its poison spikes, it must inflate its body by drinking over 2.6 gallons of water all at once.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy attached to Quilava.

flavorText: This Pokémon is fully covered by nonflammable fur. It can withstand any kind of fire attack.

attacks.name: Sleep Drain
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. Remove 4 damage counters from Parasect.

flavorText: The larger the mushroom on its back grows, the stronger the mushroom spores it scatters.

attacks.name: Moomoo Squeeze
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Moomoo Milk card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: If it is around babies, the milk it produces contains much more nutrition than usual.
It prepares for evolution by hardening its shell as much as possible to protect its soft body.

attacks.name: Group Swim
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Water Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Aqua Slash
attacks.effect: Mantine can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: As it majestically swims, it doesn't care if Remoraid attach to it for scavenging its leftovers.

attacks.name: Graffiti
attacks.effect: Igglybuff is now Asleep. During your opponent's next turn, the attack cost of each of the Defending Pokémon's attacks is Colorless more.

flavorText: Its extremely flexible and elastic body makes it bounce continuously—anytime, anywhere.

attacks.name: Green Draw
attacks.effect: Draw a card for each of your Grass Pokémon in play.

attacks.name: Double Headbutt
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: This powerful Pokémon thrusts its prized horn under its enemies' bellies, then lifts and throws them.

attacks.name: Thunder Spear
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: As a result of storing too much electricity, it developed patches where even downy wool won't grow.

attacks.name: Glare
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Fade Out
attacks.effect: Return Dunsparce and all cards attached to it to your hand.

flavorText: When spotted, this Pokémon escapes backward by furiously boring into the ground with its tail.

attacks.name: Rock Hurl
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It has sharp, hard tusks and a rugged hide. Its tackle is strong enough to knock down a house.

attacks.name: Snowy Present
attacks.effect: Draw a card for each Water Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hail
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It nests at the edge of sharp cliffs. It spends all day carrying food to its awaiting chicks.

attacks.name: Big Bite
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: If it loses a fang, a new one grows back in its place. There are always 48 fangs lining its mouth.

attacks.name: Recover
attacks.effect: Discard a Water Energy attached to Corsola and remove all damage counters from Corsola.

attacks.name: Hyper Cannon
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 50 more damage.

flavorText: It continuously sheds and grows. The tip of its head is prized as a treasure because of its beauty.

attacks.name: Energy Link
attacks.effect: Search your discard pile for an Energy card and attach it to Blissey.

attacks.name: Double-edge
attacks.effect: Blissey does 40 damage to itself.

flavorText: Anyone who takes even one taste of Blissey's egg becomes unfailingly caring and pleasant to everyone.
A spicy aroma emanates from around its neck. The aroma acts as a stimulant to restore health.

attacks.name: Super Poison Breath
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Super Explosion
attacks.effect: Weezing does 90 damage to itself, and don't apply Weakness to this damage.

flavorText: If one of the twin Koffing inflates, the other one deflates. It constantly mixes its poisonous gases.

attacks.name: Mischievous Punch
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance. Tyrogue is now Asleep.

flavorText: Even though it is small, it can't be ignored because it will slug any handy target without warning.

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy attached to Typhlosion.

flavorText: It has a secret, devastating move. It rubs its blazing fur together to cause huge explosions.
It converts sunlight into energy. In the darkness after sunset, it closes its petals and becomes still.

attacks.name: Energy Antics
attacks.effect: Move an Energy card attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon to another of your opponent's Pokémon. Smoochum is now Asleep.

flavorText: Its lips are the most sensitive part of its body. It always uses its lips first to examine things.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: If it digs at an incredible pace, it may snap off its spike and claws. They grow back in a day.

attacks.name: Playground
attacks.effect: Each player may search his or her deck for as many Basic Pokémon as he or she likes, put them onto his or her Bench, and shuffle his or her deck afterward. (You put your Pokémon on the Bench first.) Pichu is now Asleep.

flavorText: Despite its small size, it can zap even adult humans. However, if it does so, it also surprises itself.

attacks.name: Sharpen Claws
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, discard a card from your opponent's hand without looking.

attacks.name: Sneaky Attack
attacks.effect: If Persian has any Darkness Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.

flavorText: Its lithe muscles allow it to walk without making a sound. It attacks in an instant.

attacks.name: Sleep Powder
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Giant Bloom
attacks.effect: Remove 2 damage counters from Meganium.

flavorText: Meganium's breath has the power to revive dead grass and plants. It can make them healthy again.

attacks.name: Quick Draw
attacks.effect: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: The spot patterns on its back grow larger or smaller depending on the number of stars in the night sky.

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Ice Blade
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: They have gentle hearts. Because they rarely fight, many have been caught. Their number has dwindled.

attacks.name: Psychic Shot
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Always holding a pendulum that it swings at a steady rhythm, it causes drowsiness in anyone nearby.

attacks.name: Timid Tackle
attacks.effect: Granbull does 20 damage to itself. Switch Granbull with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Chomp
attacks.effect: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Granbull.

flavorText: Because its fangs are too heavy, it always keeps its head tilted down. However, its bite is powerful.

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Quick Blow
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: It makes a nest to suit its long and skinny body. The nest is impossible for other Pokémon to enter.

attacks.name: Spinning Tail
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: When it bites with its massive and powerful jaws, it shakes its head and savagely tears its victim up.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Spin Turn
attacks.effect: Switch Farfetch'd with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: If it eats the plant stalk it carries as emergency rations, it runs off in search of a new stalk.

attacks.name: Energy Absorption
attacks.effect: Search your discard pile for up to 2 Energy cards and attach them to Exeggutor.

attacks.name: Big Eggsplosion
attacks.effect: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to Exeggutor. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: If a head drops off, it emits a telepathic call in search of others to form an Exeggcute cluster.

attacks.name: Eeeeeeek
attacks.effect: Shuffle your hand into your deck, then draw 6 cards. Cleffa is now Asleep.

flavorText: Because of its unusual, star-like silhouette, people believe that it came here on a meteor.

attacks.name: Cure Powder
attacks.effect: Remove 3 damage counters from each of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Water-repellent powder on its wings enables it to collect honey, even in the heaviest of rains.

attacks.name: Leech Life
attacks.effect: Remove from Ariados the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Poisonous Saliva
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It spins string not only from its rear but also from its mouth. It's hard to tell which end is which.

attacks.name: Acceleration Bolt
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, Ampharos does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: The tail's tip shines brightly and can be seen from far away. It acts as a beacon for lost people.

attacks.name: Double Return
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage times the number of damage counters on Wobbuffet.

flavorText: It hates light and shock. If attacked, it inflates its body to build up its counterstrike.

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It has incredible intellect and intuition. Whatever the situation, it remains calm and collected.

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: The berries it stores in its vase-like shell decompose and become a gooey liquid.

attacks.name: Iron Tail
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy attached to Raichu.

flavorText: If the electric pouches in its cheeks become fully charged, both ears will stand straight up.

attacks.name: Muddy Water
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Due to its relaxed and carefree attitude, it often bumps its head on boulders and boat hulls as it swims.

attacks.name: Extrasensory
attacks.effect: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 40 damage plus 40 more damage.

flavorText: Its eyes are specially adapted. They concentrate even faint light and enable it to see in the dark.
Its nine beautiful tails are filled with a wondrous energy that could keep it alive for 1,000 years.

attacks.name: Mass Attack
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage times the number of Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's).

attacks.name: Leaf Guard
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Jumpluff by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Once it catches the wind, it deftly controls its cotton-puff spores—it can even float around the world.

attacks.name: Triple Kick
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Close Combat
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Hitmontop by attacks is increased by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It launches kicks while spinning. If it spins at high speed, it may bore its way into the ground.

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Once it appears, it goes on a rampage. It remains enraged until it demolishes everything around it.

attacks.name: Fairy Power
attacks.effect: Return 1 of your Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.

flavorText: With its acute hearing, it can pick up sounds from far away. It usually hides in quiet places.

attacks.name: Bubblebeam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: When it plays in water, it rolls up its elongated ears to prevent their insides from getting wet.
This legendary Chinese Pokémon is considered magnificent. Many people are enchanted by its grand mane.
